tweets.text:
Based on the API vs EIA running error, API was supposed to be more BEARISH than EIA on crude for this week.

Because API reported -8.33 million bbls, EIA will report between -9 to -10 million bbls.

SPR release of 8.9 million bbls.

Total crude draw of 17.9 to 18.9 million bbls, or the largest crude draw in history.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
API Inventory Moves 06/16

Crude -8.33 million (exp. -4.5 million)
Gasoline +2.479
Distillates
Cushing 

SPR actual -8.9 million (lowest levels since.... 1983)
#oott #crudeoil #gasoline #API
tweets.full_text:
Based on the API vs EIA running error, API was supposed to be more BEARISH than EIA on crude for this week.

Because API reported -8.33 million bbls, EIA will report between -9 to -10 million bbls.

SPR release of 8.9 million bbls.

Total crude draw of 17.9 to 18.9 million bbls, or the largest crude draw in history.

tweets.text:
Now today was a proper liquidation day. I didn't know if it came all at once or bled slowly over the next week (like 2018, bled for 2-weeks).

Let the burden of proof show that this MOU can reverse the deficit.

Note: November 5, 2018 saw Trump grant extensions on the Iranian oil sanctions that led to one of the largest speculator positioning washouts in oil market history. 

Oil inventories did not decline during this period.
tweets.full_text:
Now today was a proper liquidation day. I didn't know if it came all at once or bled slowly over the next week (like 2018, bled for 2-weeks).

Let the burden of proof show that this MOU can reverse the deficit.

Note: November 5, 2018 saw Trump grant extensions on the Iranian oil sanctions that led to one of the largest speculator positioning washouts in oil market history. 

Oil inventories did not decline during this period.
